verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the F1-beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase, a soluble catalytic core protein. It is a core component of the inner mitochondrial membrane-bound ATP synthase complex, residing in the mitochondrial matrix-facing portion (F1 catalytic domain). The protein is not membrane-anchored by its own TM domain or GPI anchor; it is part of a larger multi-subunit enzyme complex but does not itself reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. No documented cell-state-induced, tissue-restricted, lysosomal-exocytosis, dual-PM-localization, or stable-TM-partner-tethering biology exists for this protein. It is neither secreted nor presented as pMHC in isolation. The canonical and sole biological role is mitochondrial ATP synthesis.
